t-cyCIF

Hierarchical integration of spatial transcriptomics and proteomics could potentially enhance tumor cell resolution

Speaker notes:
By conditioning t-CycIF markers on Xenium genes, the model would potentially demonstrate robust performance even with reduced feature sets, highlighting its ability to capture subtle differences driven by post-transcriptional regulation

Single cell: QC summary

3 gels; N=5; 1 IDS & 4 PDS
QC steps included:
Demultiplexing 
Doublet removal
Low quality cells removal
Cells removed with high mitochondrial and ribosomal content

SCT + HARMONY 
SCTransform normalization to model technical noise with Harmony integration to correct for batch effects, ensuring clusters represent biological differences.

﻿﻿Based on transferring annotation from other annotated objects
﻿﻿Lineage markers based on literature

From sc data to Xenium

Subsetting the data to the 430 genes in the Xenium panel enables the separation of distinct populations.
Driving genes in each cluster at sc level also match with the spatial patterns
